apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:

    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-pdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /cockroach/cockroach
        - start
        - --insecure
        - --advertise-host=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
        - --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080
        - --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257
        - --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: COCKROACH_CHANNEL
          value: kubernetes-insecure
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 3
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          failureThreshold: 3
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF

content.thinking:
The issue is clear:
1. Pod 0 is running but not Ready because its readiness probe is failing with 503. CockroachDB needs `cockroach init` to be run after the cluster is started to initialize the cluster.
2. But also, StatefulSet only created 1 pod (pod 0), which is normal because StatefulSets by default wait for each pod to become Ready before creating the next one. Since pod 0 is not Ready, pods 1 and 2 won't be created.

The problem is a chicken-and-egg situation:
- Pod 0 can't become Ready until the cluster is initialized
- The cluster can't be initialized until all 3 pods are running
- Pods 1 and 2 won't start until pod 0 is Ready

The fix is to switch the StatefulSet to use parallel pod management so all three pods launch simultaneously, then I can run the initialization command once they're all up.

content.text: The issue is a chicken-and-egg: StatefulSet waits for pod 0 to be Ready before creating pods 1 and 2, but pod 0 can't become Ready until `cockroach init` is run, which requires all nodes to be up. The fix is `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`.
